video_title: Top #DDD RAMEN Videos with Guy Fieri 🍜 | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network

restaurants.name: 3's Bar & Grill
restaurants.owner_chef: Travis Morin, Jaron Blosser, and Cody Christopher
restaurants.guy_intro: We're at the Kihei Kalama Village, home of Fat Daddy's Barbecue, but over the way, there's a place where three surfer dudes are serving up Hawaiian, Southwestern, and Pacific Rim cuisine. I hear it's the bomb. This is 3's Bar & Grill.

dishes.description: Broth made from roasted chicken backs, local pork bones, Maui onion, carrots, daikon, celery, parsley stems, kombu, ginger, garlic, black peppercorns, dried shiitake mushrooms, simmered for 12 hours. Pork belly roasted for 6 hours with scallion, ginger, house teriyaki, sesame oil, sambal, sake, mirin, and whole garlic. Served with noodles, chicken infused soy sauce, bacon fat, kale, pickled shiitake mushrooms, raw local corn, green onions, six-minute egg, chili thread, and nori.
dishes.guy_response: That's legit. I mean, that's really legit. The noodles are spot on. The broth is the real heartbeat of this thing. And there's real depth to it. The pork belly fat, and that's the luxurious gift that really pushes this over the top. The mushrooms are the surprise factor. The chicken soy sauce is the gangster, dude. That is like the sleeper in the whole deal.

restaurants.name: Kaidama
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm here in El Paso, Texas. And just last night, I was having some fantastic ramen with my buddies. And I got done eating. But we've got to feature this on Triple D Nation. This is Kaidama.

dishes.description: Broth made from pork feet and water, boiled for 12 hours. Tare (seasoning) made with minced garlic, dashi (smoked bonito flake powder), minced ginger, salt, and sugar. Served with Sun noodles from Hawaii, scallions, uzumaki's, pork belly, and blackened garlic oil.
dishes.guy_response: It's creamy. It's rich. It coats your mouth. Your salt's right. Your seasoning's right. That's a pretty broad spectrum of flavors. I eat that ramen bowl. I am good night. Last meal I had last night, the first meal I had today.

dishes.description: Roasted chicken breast seasoned with onion, garlic, ginger, smoked paprika, and salt. Tare made from salt, sugar, smoked paprika, fire roasted tomatoes, whole garlic cloves, miso paste, water, onions, and chipotle pepper in adobo sauce, all blended. Assembled with tare, noodles, veggie broth, sweet corn, fire roasted diced tomatoes, chicken, marinated egg, sesame seeds, and chili threads.
dishes.guy_response: The chipotle bowl is my favorite. Spices are balanced out. It's like a tomatoey, chipotle kind of broth. It's delicious. It's not too spicy. It's not overwhelming. And the chicken's real tender. It's a good flavor.

restaurants.name: Boku Joe
restaurants.guy_intro: You're going to park if you want to have Amazing Ramen here in Rapid City, South Dakota. We're going to Justin Warner's new place, the wild card from Food Network, TOC. He makes the bao buns, the dumplings, and the killer ramen. This is Boku Joe.

dishes.description: Bison bones soaked for 24 hours, then cooked in a walk-in oven for 24 hours at 275 degrees with chicken feet for gelatin. Shoyu tare made in a pressure cooker with onions, garlic, ginger, MSG, kosher salt, mirin, maple syrup, black pepper, and soy sauce. Bison brisket covered with shoyu tare and water, cooked in oven at 275 for 7-8 hours. Eggs cooked 7 minutes 20 seconds, ice bathed, peeled, and soaked overnight in brown sugar, water, and soy sauce. Assembled with shoyu tare, ginger, scallion oil, broth, noodles, wilted arugula, scallions, sunflower seeds, and bison brisket.
dishes.guy_response: The broth is so bomb. It is rich. It just coats the tongue. I love so much the wilted arugula in there. It has that bitterness that comes through. Sunflower seeds are one of my favorite parts about this whole thing. The noodles are cooked perfectly. Nothing's overpowering another ingredient in the bowl. It's all balanced. The egg is so beautifully salty. It's got a hint of sweetness. Perfectly gummy.

restaurants.name: Monster Ramen
restaurants.guy_intro: Cruising into Chi-town, I got a ramen joint for you. She was on Triple G. She won big. And this is Monster Ramen.

dishes.description: House-made noodles from California flour, Canadian flour, tapioca, gluten, egg powder, water, salt, soda ash, and potassium. Chicken stock from chicken, bones, feet, and skin, pressure cooked for 1.5 hours. Beef char siu made with scallions, meat, garlic, sake, star anise, cola, brown sugar, and soy. Beef jam made from ground beef, soy sauce, water, beef shu, sake, mirin, white and black pepper, sesame oil, black bean and garlic mix, Maggi seasoning, potato starch, and dark soy. Shoyu sauce from soy paste, sweet soy sauce, fish soy sauce, and apricot extract. Soft boiled eggs brined in sake, soy sauce, mirin, star anise, and kombu for 18 hours. Assembled with sesame oil, onion oil, shoyu sauce, chicken fat, olive oil, white pepper, crushed garlic, noodles, chicken stock, bamboo shoots, wood ear mushroom, chopped leeks, red onions, beef jam, beef char siu, ramen egg, house pepper mix, sesame seed, and garlic.
dishes.guy_response: Oh. That beef jam. I would be here every day. Chef, this is unbelievable. I've had a lot of dynamite ramens. This is Michelin star ramen. Wow. Astounding how much detail. But it tastes so warm and homey, and it doesn't taste pretentious. I'm in awe. This is one of the most exercised flexes in culinary that I've seen on Triple D.

restaurants.name: Toki Underground
restaurants.guy_intro: Hanging out here at the Toki Underground in D.C. I just had probably the most amazing vegetarian dumplings you could ever imagine. So, since we just start off with a brief appetizer, what's next?

dishes.dish_name: Toki Classic Ramen with Pulled Pork and Chicken Oysters
dishes.description: Pulled pork made from pork shoulder braised for 5 hours at 325 degrees with low-sodium soy sauce, mirin, Chinese cooking wine, and Chinese five-spice blend. Chicken oysters harvested from chicken and seared. Triple stock combining pork stock (pork bones and water), dashi stock (kombu, bonito flakes, mackerel teabag mix), and chicken stock (garlic, ginger, onions, carrots, chicken carcass). Assembled with braising liquid (pork fat and braising liquid from pork butt), service broth, noodles, locally grown bok choy, pulled pork, scallions, house-made pickled ginger, sesame seeds, crispy chicken oysters, slow poached egg, chili oil, and black garlic oil.
dishes.guy_response: What really I'm digging right now, I just got a little kick of, is the chili oil with the garlic sauce. Tremendous amount of depth. Little bit of rich, little bit of tang, little bit of chew. And I didn't even crack the egg and let that come out. It is truly a masterpiece of food.

restaurants.name: Kobe Japanese Restaurant
restaurants.guy_intro: Triple D hanging out in Salt Lake City, Utah at a funky joint called Kobe Japanese Restaurant. This wild man with his wife, they've had this dynamite place for the last eight and a half years?

dishes.description: Cold ramen dish. Char siu pork made from pork belly cooked in stock pot/broth for 4-6 hours, then braised for 4 hours in soy sauce, sake, pork broth, mirin, salt, grated garlic, ginger, and water. Sesame sauce made from water, soy sauce, vinegar, sesame oil, sugar, stock, and sesame paste. Assembled with pan-seared char siu, cold noodles, asparagus, pickled bean sprouts (in rice vinegar), egg, cucumbers, and spicy mustard.
dishes.guy_response: Straight to your sinuses. That's full-on hot mustard. You're just trying to drive me crazy. Japanese food is really about balance, really about technique, really about appreciating the ingredients. And that's one of the things that you are doing so well here. And that noodle dish right there, as legit and as delicious as a tamporo. Dynamite. Well done. Those noodles are cold and ready to go. It has this rich, creamy, peanut butter flavor. And with the cucumbers and the asparagus, it's delicious.

restaurants.name: Bluebird Barbecue
restaurants.guy_intro: Welcome back, hanging out in Burlington, Vermont, at the Bluebird barbecue joint. And I'll give you some props, man. You're pulling it off in style, my friend.

dishes.dish_name: Pit Master Ramen with Burnt Ends
dishes.description: Burnt ends made from brisket seasoned with salt and pepper, smoked for 15 hours, then chopped into cubes, tossed with pit master rub and in-house Texas-style barbecue sauce, and smoked for another hour. Broth made from chicken backs and necks, onions, carrots, celery, fresh thyme, bay leaves, black peppercorns, and water, simmered for 12 hours. Dashi made with ginger, garlic, lemongrass, soy sauce, bonito flakes, kombu, cilantro, green onions, and lime juice. Assembled with pre-blanched Sun Ramen Company noodles, dashi broth, six-minute egg, in-house pickled carrot, in-house pickled jalapeno, shaved radishes, chopped scallions, burnt ends, fresh cilantro, and toasted sesame oil.
dishes.guy_response: The dashi broth is spot on. And it's actually a little more aggressive than maybe a typical dashi because you're going to drop in these big pieces of burnt end, which I was so worried. We're just going to turn into a smoke pit, but it doesn't. Absolutely not. Love the acid of the pickling and a really good noodle. Legit, bro. Noodles going in. The meat is so tender and juicy. It's got that umami, the whole sweet and the sour and deliciousness.
